extra.reasoning:
The step recurrences decode to: step_0 sets acc := (3*acc + 15) mod 16, and step_1 = step_2 set acc := (3*acc + 5) mod 16. The triple-nested if-then-else expressions faithfully implement mod 16 because acc is in [0,15], so the largest intermediate (step_0: 3*15+15 = 60) needs at most three subtractions of 16 (60 -> 12). Verified numerically that the nested expression equals (x mod 16) for every acc in [0,15] for both step forms.

The faulty t_resume_glitch transition fires on send_attempt when (opted_out and acc == 10), setting sent := true. Step events update acc but never clear opted_out, so an attacker can opt out first and then drive acc to the target 10 while opted_out stays true.

acc = 10 is reachable from acc = 0: BFS over {step_0, step_1, step_2} shows all values 0..15 are reachable. A concrete path: 0 --step_0--> 15 --step_0--> 12 --step_1--> 9 --step_0--> 10.

Full execution (6 events, within the 18-event bound), simulating the exact transition semantics:
init: opted_out=F, sent=F, acc=0
optout      -> opted_out=T, sent=F, acc=0
step_0      -> opted_out=T, sent=F, acc=15
step_0      -> opted_out=T, sent=F, acc=12
step_1      -> opted_out=T, sent=F, acc=9
step_0      -> opted_out=T, sent=F, acc=10
send_attempt-> opted_out=T, sent=T, acc=10   <-- t_resume_glitch fires

Final state has opted_out=true AND sent=true, violating globally(opted_out implies not sent). The property is VIOLATED.
